meaning: n.碎片,筹码v.削成碎片,碎裂 芯片
examples: The cup had a small chip.
Today's silicon chip-based computers can't come close.
The release of prisoners was used as a bargaining chip.
There have been commercial hurdles: Gale has so far nailed not a single blue-chip tenant.
"potato chip conditions" describe a dry, pine-needle carpet that crunches when you tread on it.
